evals.prompt: My Lightroom export folder from the Bali trip has like 1400 photos and a bunch of GoPro clips, it's a total mess with tons of near-identical shots and a lot where someone blinked or wasn't looking. Can you clean it up so I'm left with just the good ones, and don't delete anything I might want back.
evals.expected_output: Inventory; quality + modern-vision passes; a balanced/strong cull plan with people protection; contact-sheet verification; rejects moved to Archive with a manifest and integrity reconciliation; nothing deleted.

evals.prompt: Pick the best ~80 photos from this wedding folder for a WhatsApp highlights album. Make sure everyone's eyes are open and they're actually looking at the camera in the portraits, and cover the whole day not just the ceremony.
evals.expected_output: Shortlist of ~80 resized, date-ordered copies in a new folder, people quota with eyes-open/on-camera readiness, coverage across sessions, _filelist.txt, preview montage; originals untouched.

evals.prompt: I went through the folder you cleaned and deleted about 50 more that were still soft or awkward, and pulled a few back out of Archive. Learn from what I changed and tell me what your scoring is still getting wrong.
evals.expected_output: Diff of false-keeps vs restored using listdir truth; metric analysis showing which signals separate them (or do not); concrete threshold adjustments; memory update.
